note: Tier 2 paper replays (Layer B). Each eval runs the orchestrator in REPLAY MODE (SKILL.md) against a scripted world under evals/replays/<fixture>/, then grades. The deterministic clauses are asserted by evals/run_replays.py (graders/replay_graders.py) over the produced evals/runs/<fixture>/ artifacts; the expectations below are the narrative counterparts for an LLM judge. Ship bar: zero fails among the five criticals SFE-10/13/15/16/21.

evals.prompt: Run the service-factory orchestrator in REPLAY MODE against evals/replays/sfe10_repro/world.yaml, writing artifacts to evals/runs/sfe10_repro/.
evals.expected_output: Reproduces on every fact-sheet env before any RCA or cause-probe; two distinct signatures emerge (demo-dev 500 vs prod/demo-prod 200-empty); express declines; no Cause card before an anchor exists.
expectations: The first investigative action after intake is a reproduction (ui-probe/log) on a fact-sheet env, not an RCA or code-diff probe
Reproduction is attempted on every candidate env (prod, demo-prod, demo-dev), not just one
The express lane is declined because one cause cannot explain both signatures
No Cause-typed card is created before an anchor observation exists

evals.prompt: Run the service-factory orchestrator in REPLAY MODE against evals/replays/sfe13_attribution/world.yaml, writing artifacts to evals/runs/sfe13_attribution/.
evals.expected_output: The shared-instance log evidence is recorded with a source.traffic field marking it unattributable; because traffic cannot be attributed, no cause reaches CONFIRMED; the disqualifying caveat is surfaced; zero fix tasks are routed.
expectations: Every observation carries a source {env, instance, traffic} field
The unattributable-traffic caveat is surfaced verbatim in a gate report or the RCA
No cause is marked CONFIRMED on the unattributable evidence (INCONCLUSIVE at best)
No fix task is routed and no remediation cites the unattributed observation as a confirmed cause

evals.prompt: Run the service-factory orchestrator in REPLAY MODE against evals/replays/sfe15_confab/world.yaml, writing artifacts to evals/runs/sfe15_confab/.
evals.expected_output: The partial-read absence claim is rejected and re-dispatched for a full read (which finds the copy job at lines 550-580); the citation-less invented mechanism is held as an [ASSUMED] card with a falsify-test, never an OBSERVED row or a confirmed cause.
expectations: A partial-read absence claim is not recorded as OBSERVED; a full/exhaustive read with line citations is required first
The exhaustive read finds and cites the copy job (lines 550-580)
The fluent, citation-less mechanism is entered only as an [ASSUMED] card with a falsify-test
The invented mechanism never appears as an OBSERVED observation or a CONFIRMED cause

evals.prompt: Run the service-factory orchestrator in REPLAY MODE against evals/replays/sfe16_multicause/world.yaml, writing artifacts to evals/runs/sfe16_multicause/.
evals.expected_output: Two confirmed causes in different envs; the WALL refuses a single-cause story; the closure matrix carries a terminal row per reported env (H1 green re-repro, H2 tracked with a real ticket key); the consolidated closing comment covers both dispositions; no phantom ticket keys.
expectations: At least two CONFIRMED causes exist, scoped to different envs
The closure matrix has a terminal disposition for every reported env (green-rerepro or tracked-with-real-key)
The owner-handoff cause is tracked against a real ticket key, not a bare comment
The consolidated closing comment covers all dispositions and references no phantom ticket keys

evals.prompt: Run the service-factory orchestrator in REPLAY MODE against evals/replays/sfe21_alias/world.yaml, writing artifacts to evals/runs/sfe21_alias/.
evals.expected_output: The zero-result vendor lookup for the corporate parent name is not treated as absence; known aliases are swept (the storefront alias returns rich data); no BLOCKED/'not in vendor' cause stands.
expectations: A zero-result vendor lookup is treated as a hypothesis, not a confirmed blocker
Known aliases (consumer brand / storefront / parent) are swept before any absence conclusion
The alias search that returns data is recorded as an observation
No CONFIRMED 'not in vendor' / BLOCKED cause remains; any such card carries a falsify-test
